name: Daily content journal — the reservoir that feeds public surfaces
next_move: Stand up Journal subsystem at _ledger/journal/ with one file per day (YYYY-MM-DD.md). Each entry: title, body, optional attachments (screenshots, video clips, diagrams) stored alongside. Tags field so entries retrieve by framework area, platform-intent (twitter-thread-candidate / youtube-explainer-candidate / linkedin-post-candidate), and media type. Journal view in Secretary shows entries chronologically with a pull-by-tag filter. Optional: cheap-model reflection pass that auto-suggests tags and which surface an entry belongs on.
blockers: Tag vocabulary — controlled list vs freeform with later reconciliation
Attachment storage path convention (_ledger/journal/attachments/ vs inline per-day folder)
Whether the reflection pass runs on write or on-demand
live_band: Right now every discovery gets mentioned in a chat, lives in a voice note, or disappears into a Codex session. The reservoir pattern captures every discovery at the moment it happens, even if it takes weeks before it gets shaped into a public artifact. Separating capture from publication is what makes the content flywheel work — can't publish what wasn't captured, and capture pressure shouldn't demand publication-readiness. Closes a loop with public_surface_build — journal is what feeds Twitter/YouTube/LinkedIn when those go live.
notes: Declared 2026-04-22 in Jake's scope riff. Comparator hint: reservoir_building. Weight 0.80 — the daily cost is low and the compounding value into public-surface work is high. Probably the single most valuable streak in the set.
